title: Constipation follow-up in GP
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Lists new onset in older people, anaemia, rectal bleeding, family history, tenesmus, weight loss, and persistent unresponsive constipation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Constipation – 04
source.population: People presenting with constipation; no precise age range is stated in the applicability section.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth constipation documentation; partial for a setting-agnostic GP workflow.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies nature, onset, duration, bowel diary, secondary causes, straining, incomplete evacuation, abdominal pain, bleeding, and medication context.

assessment.text: Specifies nature, onset, duration, bowel diary, secondary causes, straining, incomplete evacuation, abdominal pain, bleeding, and medication context.
assessment.rationale: The exact DHA history section supports interval bowel pattern, onset, duration, diary, secondary causes, straining, incomplete evacuation, abdominal pain, bleeding, medicines, and change since review without confirming constipation cause.

assessment.text: Lists new onset in older people, anaemia, rectal bleeding, family history, tenesmus, weight loss, and persistent unresponsive constipation.
assessment.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed older-age onset, anaemia, bleeding, family history, tenesmus, weight loss, persistent symptoms, examination, and escalation concern without asserting absence.

assessment.text: Limits laboratory and colorectal imaging evaluation to persistent, unresponsive, or suspected secondary disease.
assessment.rationale: The exact investigation section supports recording already ordered laboratory and colorectal evaluation context without generating a test.
